reviews.review_description: Very fascinating game so far , enjoyable with some great eye candy oit in space I too would prefer normal controls split between both controllers its very odd and awkward to move about turning and walking on one controller only , i find myself wanting to slide over and turn a bit and it spins me around too much when i wanted to move forward a few feet

developer_response.body:
Hi Greg,
We love feedback, its the only way to improve.
Per your note about the turns, we have an option in the settings menu that allows you to customize this.  If you get the chance, please try and let us know if that helps.

PS if you use discord, we can help you through this and find out secrets and easter eggs to learn across your journey

https://discord.com/invite/KFdudUmWx7

reviews.review_description: I would love for this game to work but it won't actually let me complete the missions in the beginning where I have to get rid of ice rot and such. You also can't save the game so I've had to restart already one. Honestly save your money though until they fix some bugs and actually allow you to play the game... It's not worth it. I would love to be able to give a higher star review, And I'm more than open to the idea of editing this review if things get fixed.

developer_response.body:
Hi IzRaina, thanks for taking the time to leave this feedback.

We’re really sorry you ran into mission-blocking issues. That’s definitely not the experience we want anyone to have and we'd like to learn more. We’re continuously working on fixes for our next version, and it would be great to understand your issues further (as this appears to be a new bug or something we can improve with information).  

Regarding saving...the game autosaves currently at the end of each chapter [so you don't have to].  You can also replay any section already completed. The ability to go to a saved or completed section can be found in the START screen in the space ship.  Can you please try this, and if this doesn't work, let us know and we'll explore.

If you’re open to it, we’d love for you to reach out to us at epvr@mxtreality.com or our discord https://discord.com/invite/KFdudUmWx7

We truly appreciate that you’re willing to update your review once things improve. We’re committed to getting it there.

PS any folks that give us considerable useful feedback are immortalized with a personalized Easter Egg!

reviews.review_title: Very unique and enjoyable VR game
reviews.review_description: I got EPVR for my Q2.   I'm a couple weeks shy of being 75 and thoroughly enjoyed the challenges of exploring Europa.   It was a little puzzling at first but thanks to the wonderful developers who responded to me on Discord with small hints,  I was able to work through the problem spots on my own.   I love puzzle/adventure games and this fit the bill perfectly.   There wasn't any battles with aliens which was a plus.  It would be a wonderful game for young people as it is STEM oriented.      I am looking forward to the next chapter!

I completed the game so I got to see all the puzzles and most of the world (things have been added that I need to go check out). It was really convincing to experience a vertically spread base as required to be both on the surface and reaching liquid ocean below the ice on Europa. It is fun to see how we imagine this exploration really happening.

Parts of the game also require a virtual view of a more distant location and that is also great to engage with. There is a lot of potential for more and I know more things are being added.

I loved the setting in the future that had its roots in real space exploration events around Jupiter and added the speculative work required to build a full base on Europa.

The puzzles incorporate engineering principles while keeping it light and fun. For example, one requires the use of mirrors to manipulate the beam from a laser. It makes me think of how splitting beams and mirroring are used to do real physics experiments.

See you all on Europa.

Haven't gotten too far yet but so far its good.

Game looks good as long you use Snap turn or turn IRL as the game stutters if you try using smooth turn. And I'm not a fan of the controls, having Forward/Back and Turn on the same stick causes you turn slightly when you really just want to go forward. 

But the puzzles and environment are great so far

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for playing and sharing your thoughts! We’re thrilled you’re enjoying the puzzles and environment so far.

Regarding the controls, we completely understand your feedback, and it's a good time to explain the background as to why we went against the common approach. Instead of building a game and making it accessible, we tried to consider game play and control dynamics from the foundation.  After extensive testing with players of different ages, backgrounds, VR experience levels, and disabilities, we discovered that standard VR control schemes were unintuitive for many users (as each hand performs different tasks - which ends up sending most players into the wall on the first sessions and getting frustrated and pulling the headset off). Through this research, we found that mirroring controls across both hands made movement and interaction feel more natural and accessible to most, as it reduces confusion and helps players stay immersed in the world.  Our dev team are big gamers, and we disagreed with this at first, yet after playing this way where amazed how quickly we adapted and now prefer this system.

That said, we have a plan on our road map to go the extra mile, and add customizable control options in a future update so everyone can fine-tune movement to their own comfort and play style. 

Accessibility and intuitive design are core to this project, and your feedback helps us keep improving.

Thank you again for playing Europa Prime... we hope you keep exploring and enjoy what’s ahead!

PS as you were our first feedback, we will soon be adding a special easter egg in your honor.  Pls check our socials, discord, website, or email us for details.

reviews.review_description: Game environment looks gorgeous, and the puzzle and exploration elements are engaging, with a good level of challenge — have to think and try things, but not frustrating. Controllers can take a bit of getting used to, as the functions are the same on both left and right, but work well once figure that out (plus I transport instead of glide) and easy to explain to others. Love the Europa space setting.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words... we’re thrilled you’re enjoying the puzzles, exploration, and Europa settings of #EPVR! 

You’re absolutely right, the mirrored controls can take a moment to get used to, but we designed them that way after user user testing to make interactions feel more intuitive and accessible for players of all backgrounds and abilities. It’s great to hear that they worked well once you got the hang of them!

We really appreciate you taking the time to share your experience.  Feedback like this helps us keep refining Europa Prime and shaping the future of accessible VR gameplay that benefits everyone.

reviews.review_description:
Recently I finished my first playthrough of EPVR, which was enjoyable from start to finish.

Needing to learn about Europa to complete the puzzles was an smart and engaging way to incorporate learning with fun. There was never a dull moment in playing this game.

I've always loved what MXTreality stood for as a company, and this game really shows their dedication to innovation in education within VR. Even though EPVR is still in early access, it already feels polished and engaging in it's current state. Bonus for all of the little Easter eggs you can find, very fun looking for them and adds to the replay value. 

Overall, EPVR is a fantastic experience that blends the learning of space and puzzle-solving beautifully, I can't wait to see where MXTreality takes the game next.

Lasty, special shout out to all of the developers, play testers, and everyone else who helped make this game possible. ❤️

I'm finding this game very unique and interesting.  The environment is awesome!  I love doing puzzle type games, even though i'm not too great at them.  I'm still working my way through and hopefully I can figure it all out.  :)
I really like how interactive the experience is. You are learning about Europa and while doing that you are finding hints to solve puzzles and unlock the next parts of the experience.
The game delivers a solid experience of puzzle-solving and cool environments!
